data.content:
Here's a summary of every legacy pattern identified and what was changed:

| Legacy Pattern | What Changed |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | → `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` attribute |
| `<Import ...Microsoft.Common.props>` | Removed — SDK imports this automatically |
| `<Import ...Microsoft.CSharp.targets>` | Removed — SDK imports this automatically |
| `Configuration`/`Platform` conditionals | Removed — SDK provides sensible defaults |
| `Debug|AnyCPU` / `Release|AnyCPU` groups | Removed — SDK defaults match (debug symbols, optimize, output paths, `DEBUG;TRACE` constants) |
| `<TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</...>` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| `ProjectGuid`, `OutputType` (Library), `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic`, `ErrorReport`, `WarningLevel` | Removed — all are SDK defaults |
| Explicit `<Reference>` items (System, System.Core, etc.) | Removed — implicit for `net472` |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Removed — SDK uses file globbing (`**/*.cs`) |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Deleted — attributes moved to `<PropertyGroup>` properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Version`, etc.) |

The project file went from **47 lines → 16 lines** with identical build behavior.
